steps.title: Use a restrictive appositive
steps.content: The blank completes the phrase "of keen interest to [linguist] of the University of Toronto." The name Diane Massam identifies which linguist is meant, so it functions as a restrictive appositive. A restrictive appositive is not separated from the noun it identifies by punctuation.

steps.title: Rule out punctuation errors
steps.content:
• Choice A: A colon cannot follow "of keen interest to linguist" because that wording is not an independent clause.
  • Choice B: Commas around the name treat it as optional extra information, which does not fit the identifying role here.
  • Choice D: A single comma before the name creates an unsupported break between the noun and the identifying appositive.
The answer is C.
